essence: AWB proof-of-concept inside prompt.supportsignal must be rebuilt as a standalone AppyStack app with autopilot, human-in-the-loop hooks, a UI-separated workflow object model, shared workflow memory, and API endpoints instead of the Claude harness.

actions.text: Rebuild Agent Workflow Builder as a standalone application on AppyStack with unit tests
Separate the workflow object model (nodes/schemas/UI definition) from any specific UI so it can embed in host apps like the support app
Add autopilot with optional non-blocking nodes and refactor to work via API endpoints instead of the Claude harness

essence: Three AngelEye problems catalogued: BMAD's seven-session workflows don't reliably surface (edge sessions missing), no documented/repeatable data-discovery-pattern system (Archon might automate it), and the ingestion framework needs an isolated crash-proof JSON/HTTP endpoint running from system startup.

actions.text: Fix AngelEye session discovery so edge sessions of the 7-session BMAD workflow register reliably
Document data discovery patterns plus a repeatable pattern-rediscovery process (evaluate Archon for automation)
Isolate the ingestion framework as a robust JSON/HTTP endpoint that starts at boot, separate from the crash-prone web app

essence: Watched a Dynamous livestream previewing Archon — workflows packaged as the engine for planning/implementation/code review orchestrated from a second brain, with public release targeted the following Wednesday.

Chiang Mai meetup demo: David walked through AngelEye visualizing BMAD workflows (Scrum Master/Amelia/Nate/Taylor/Lisa agents), repurposed Paperclip as an autonomous harness to analyze thousands of Claude session logs with CEO/CTO/UX-designer agents plus the Impeccable skill bundle, trialed Hermes as a terminal-first memory-strong assistant, and discussed the Anthropic CLI leak spawning future harness-based orchestrators.

Live working session with Lars debugging his OMI plugin pipeline — quick-brown-fox capture verified, plugin version drift found (2.3.0 vs 2.4.4), Gemini bad-JSON extraction failures observed, live-session archiving confirmed working, first successful two-way external agent communication celebrated.

actions.text: Harden OMI extraction against Gemini bad-JSON so the pipeline heals instead of stopping after one retry

essence: Designed a file-based instruction loop for Lars: David writes instructions to a file that Lars's agent polls every minute, machine-readable (JSONL/CSV/markdown) with check-off-on-completion and per-instruction handling metadata.

actions.text: Implement one-minute-loop instruction file (JSONL/CSV) with completion check-off and handling metadata for Lars's agent

essence: Pre-session review of the OMI automation setup for Lars — flagged the brains-index file Lars won't have, a token-burning issue to fix, questions on Omi Service Install scheduling/audit logging, and requested a sustainable folder structure for daily education sessions building shared brain knowledge.

actions.text: Fix the token-burning issue in the OMI automation file setup
Add the server log file to .gitignore
Design a sustainable folder structure for recurring education sessions and accumulating shared brain knowledge

essence: Epic triage: 5.9 and 5.1 relate to the paused Agent Workflow Builder wizards work (Epic 3-ish); David agreed with option A on where to place the remaining AWB work.

Decided the OMI script should heal gracefully when brains_index.json is missing on Lars's machine, get 1-2 retries for Gemini bad-JSON, live in shared memory short-term (repo long-term), and keep the unneeded claude -p post-processing step as a commented section.

actions.text: Add healing fallback for missing brains_index.json plus 1-2 Gemini retry attempts to the OMI script
Place omiscript in the shared memory system now and plan a proper repository home

essence: Questioned whether omi-fetch and art-fetch are deterministic or have nondeterministic (Claude/Gemini) aspects — arguing shell-script invocation of nondeterministic skills is fragile and asking for an architecture review before building a new epic from planning docs.

actions.text: Review omi-fetch/art-fetch architecture to separate deterministic execution from nondeterministic AI steps (possibly via claude -p)

essence: Team retro on a workflow session: previously abstract work turned into a structured synchronized system, 'viral' and 'scout' roles clarified, Mary repositioned to the admissions/intake side, and future sessions to be shorter and execution-driven.

Pitched an AI-driven storyline/previs app — timeline editor meets Disney storyboard with transcript, per-second emotion/sound tracks, and scrubbable image pillars edited before video generation — to be built on early-access Anthropic tech, with Kip validating it against real film previs/LED-wall (Unity) workflows and the Vos/Byron advisor-agent layer wired via shared memory.

actions.text: Build a requirements document for the storyline/previs application to feed the early-access Anthropic app-builder
